title: Sarcocephalus cadamba
taxonomy: Sarcocephalus cadamba is classified under the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family consists of over 13,000 species, making it one of the largest families of flowering plants. Within the family Rubiaceae, Sarcocephalus cadamba is further classified into the genus Sarcocephalus. The genus Sarcocephalus is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa, and Sarcocephalus cadamba is one of the species within this genus. It is a deciduous tree that is widely distributed across tropical Africa.

intro: Sarcocephalus cadamba, commonly known as the Cadamba tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to tropical Asia. The Cadamba tree has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
